jokes.text: Oh, did you pick a special outfit... So you can look sexy shooting pool?
jokes.setup: Jeff mentions starting billiards class
jokes.punchline: Shirley immediately assumes Jeff's motivation is vanity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Shirley knows Jeff well enough to immediately call out his vanity. Sets up the episode's central conflict about appearance vs. substance.

jokes.text: 13. - Ah, great, so much for 'baggels.'
jokes.setup: Troy drops the bagels after saying '13'
jokes.punchline: Britta mispronounces 'bagels' as 'baggels' in her complaint
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag of Britta's mispronunciation. The '13' suggests superstition or counting, adding layers.

jokes.text: The baggels. You dropped them on the floor. Uh, they're called bagels. I lived in new york, troy. I know what a baggel is.
jokes.setup: Troy mispronounces 'bagels' as 'baggels'
jokes.punchline: Britta corrects him while mispronouncing it the exact same way, despite claiming New York credibility
jokes.explanation: Excellent example of Britta's oblivious hypocrisy - she's completely unaware she's making the same mistake while being condescending about it.

jokes.text: What the hell is wrong with you? What? You say bagel wrong. I say it the same as you. Say it again. Baggel.
jokes.setup: Jeff confronts Britta about her pronunciation
jokes.punchline: Britta remains completely oblivious, repeating the mispronunciation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the bagel bit. Britta's complete lack of self-awareness is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: What 'padge' was that? Two 'five -ty' eight.
jokes.setup: The pronunciation issue spreading to other words
jokes.punchline: Jeff sarcastically mimics the mispronunciation pattern, Troy obliviously continues it
jokes.explanation: Smart escalation that shows how linguistic quirks can spread. Jeff's sarcasm paired with Troy's earnestness creates good contrast.

jokes.text: Strictly speaking, troy, The bible condemns this level of friendship.
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed's close friendship being discussed
jokes.punchline: Shirley's matter-of-fact biblical condemnation of male friendship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley line - her casual homophobia delivered with religious authority. Dark but perfectly in character and era-appropriate.

jokes.text: It's just like the notebook, accept, instead of alzheimer's, Abed has-- ahem! Someone who likes him.
jokes.setup: Annie comparing the situation to The Notebook
jokes.punchline: She catches herself before saying something mean about Abed's social prospects
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Annie's attempt at kindness while being accidentally cruel. The self-correction is believable.

jokes.text: Well, whoever she is, she's fat. These thick pencil lines are drawn with a heavy hand.
jokes.setup: Group speculating about the mystery girl
jokes.punchline: Pierce's absurd forensic analysis leading to a cruel conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful Pierce logic - taking something innocent and making it cruel through pseudo-scientific reasoning.

jokes.text: Unless it's a boy- -then we have to find him for jesus.
jokes.setup: Discussion about finding Abed's admirer
jokes.punchline: Shirley's casual homophobia disguised as religious concern
jokes.explanation: Another perfect Shirley moment - her prejudice delivered with such matter-of-fact religiosity that it's both offensive and funny.

jokes.text: Guys! Class is over. I'm trying to get you to leave. Oh...Cool. Pick that up.
jokes.setup: Students keep saying 'Adios' not realizing class is over
jokes.punchline: Chang's frustrated directness followed by students' casual compliance
jokes.explanation: Decent Chang moment showing his exasperation with students. The casual 'pick that up' adds a nice authoritarian touch.

jokes.text: If all chinese were psychic, you would have started... Using birth control centuries ago.
jokes.setup: Students amazed at Chang's ability to know the girl's name
jokes.punchline: Chang's self-deprecating racist joke about Chinese population
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang - turning racism against himself while being genuinely clever. Dark but delivered with perfect timing.

jokes.text: I may have been thrown off... By britta's pronunciation of the word 'bagel.'
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining why he didn't think of checking the name in the book
jokes.punchline: Blaming his mental lapse on Britta's mispronunciation
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Jeff's vanity - he can't admit to a simple oversight so blames Britta's pronunciation.

jokes.text: How do you pronounce bagel? I don't. Come on. Baggel. Ugh! You're the worst.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to get Britta to say bagel correctly
jokes.punchline: She says it wrong again, Jeff's exasperated 'You're the worst'
jokes.explanation: 'You're the worst' becomes an iconic Jeff-to-Britta line. Perfect encapsulation of their dynamic.

jokes.text: See? It's a fat girl's name. Might as well be gravy jones.
jokes.setup: Learning the girl's name is Jenny Adams
jokes.punchline: Pierce's cruel logic about names predicting body types
jokes.explanation: Awful Pierce logic that's somehow convincing in its internal consistency. 'Gravy Jones' is an inspired fake fat name.

jokes.text: Abed, for guys like you, This kind of opportunity only comes around... Once in a li-- While.
jokes.setup: Pierce giving Abed dating advice
jokes.punchline: Pierce catches himself before saying 'lifetime' to spare Abed's feelings
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment where Pierce's attempt at kindness makes things worse. The pause and correction are perfectly timed.

jokes.text: A time when a simple pillow fight... Between two innocent girls could turn into a steamy night... Of unbridled lust. Britta knows all about it.
jokes.setup: Pierce describing college as a time of exploration
jokes.punchline: His creepy sexual fantasy ending with an accusation about Britta
jokes.explanation: Typical Pierce creepiness. Gets points for character consistency but it's a bit predictable and overlong.

jokes.text: So you guys are gonna can't buy me love me, right? We're gonna what you? You're gonna can't buy me love me.
jokes.setup: Abed wanting a makeover
jokes.punchline: Using movie titles as verbs, Annie's confusion at the reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - turning movie references into practical vocabulary. Very meta and character-specific.

jokes.text: Oh, he wants us to love don't cost a thing him. Can't buy me love was the remake for white audiences.
jokes.setup: Continuation of the movie-title-as-verb concept
jokes.punchline: Abed's casual racial analysis of Hollywood remakes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Abed moment combining movie knowledge with casual social commentary. The off-hand delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That's so uncomfortable when they do that. I can't believe it doesn't insult them.
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed's casual discussion of racial Hollywood dynamics
jokes.punchline: Annie's discomfort with their matter-of-fact racial awareness
jokes.explanation: Good awkward white person moment. Annie's discomfort at casual racial discussion is very believable.

jokes.text: Sure glad there are no old people here. This conversation would probably be total gibberish to them.
jokes.setup: Complex discussion of movie references and racial dynamics
jokes.punchline: Pierce, the oldest person there, commenting on how confusing it would be for old people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce obliviousness. He's completely unaware he's the old person who doesn't get it. Great irony.

jokes.text: Yeah, and then you can take her to a nice deli... And treat her to a baggel.
jokes.setup: Group encouraging Abed about dating
jokes.punchline: Britta still mispronouncing bagel while trying to be supportive
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that reinforces Britta's obliviousness. She remains completely unaware of her mispronunciation.

jokes.text: You're gonna look like an ass in those. Shut up, leonard. I talked to your son on family day. I know all about your gambling.
jokes.setup: Leonard mocking Jeff about the gym shorts
jokes.punchline: Jeff's devastating personal attack using insider information
jokes.explanation: Great Jeff moment - using his social intelligence as a weapon. The specificity of 'family day' makes it feel real and cruel.

jokes.text: Whoa! That's her? She's pretty! Wh-which is not a surprise at all, abed.
jokes.setup: Troy seeing Jenny is attractive
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to cover his surprise that Abed could attract someone pretty
jokes.explanation: Good awkward Troy moment. His attempt at damage control makes his initial surprise more obvious.

jokes.text: What are you doing? - I'm being myself. Go be yourself by jenny.
jokes.setup: Group tells Abed to 'be himself'
jokes.punchline: Abed literally interprets this as staying where he is
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed literal-mindedness. Shows his social awkwardness through logical interpretation.

jokes.text: But I wouldn't go over there. How do you know that? A lifetime of observation, mostly.
jokes.setup: Asked why he wouldn't approach the girl
jokes.punchline: Abed's matter-of-fact self-awareness about his social limitations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed - clinical self-awareness delivered deadpan. Shows his intelligence and self-knowledge without self-pity.

jokes.text: What the hell was that? A different version of me. I think it was a vampire.
jokes.setup: Abed's failed attempt at being someone else
jokes.punchline: His clinical analysis that he was channeling a vampire
jokes.explanation: Great Abed moment - his meta-analysis of his own behavior is both funny and revealing of his character.

jokes.text: I-I-I--it's like abed, but employable.
jokes.setup: Group sees 'white Abed' (Joey)
jokes.punchline: Troy's cruel but accurate observation about racial employment dynamics
jokes.explanation: Dark but incisive social commentary delivered as casual observation. Uncomfortable truth made funny.

jokes.text: Do you have something against playing pool... With a full range of motion and optimal comfort? I'm comfortable in my own clothes.
jokes.setup: Argument about wearing shorts for pool
jokes.punchline: Coach's overwrought justification vs. Jeff's simple preference
jokes.explanation: Sets up the central conflict. Coach's intensity about shorts is appropriately absurd but not exceptionally funny.

jokes.text: I said, I bet you're comfortable... Dressed like a model instead of an athlete, Sippin' martinis and smokin'... Instead of keeping your game on the table!
jokes.setup: Coach getting worked up about Jeff's appearance
jokes.punchline: His overwrought speech about martinis and smoking vs. pool purity
jokes.explanation: Coach's intensity about pool purity is funny but feels a bit forced. The imagery is vivid but overlong.

jokes.text: This hipster is dropping this class. Because he cares more about what he looks like... Than how he's playing.
jokes.setup: Coach's assumption about Jeff's motivations
jokes.punchline: The irony that he's obsessing over appearance (shorts) while accusing Jeff of vanity
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment - Coach is projecting his own appearance obsession onto Jeff. Shows the hypocrisy well.

jokes.text: Vanity, thy name is... His name. It's the first day. I didn't catch it.
jokes.setup: Jeff starting a dramatic Shakespeare reference
jokes.punchline: Having to awkwardly admit he doesn't know Coach's name
jokes.explanation: Great Jeff moment - his pretentious Shakespeare reference deflated by practical reality. Perfect character beat.

jokes.text: I understand--I need to change who I am... To someone more likable. No, no, no, no, sweetie. It's not about changing. It's about learning. Learning to change?
jokes.setup: Annie trying to explain the difference between changing and learning
jokes.punchline: Abed's logical follow-up that exposes the contradiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - his logical mind cuts through euphemistic language to the truth underneath.

jokes.text: Because I can't think of anything more frightening... Than a half-polish, half-arab virgin in his 30s. One way or the other, That story ends with an explosion.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining why Abed needs to change
jokes.punchline: His racist/xenophobic assumption about Abed's ethnic background and future
jokes.explanation: Dark Pierce humor that's very much of its time. Offensive but delivered in character. Not particularly clever, just shocking.

jokes.text: So you're familiar with two sins. How about a third?
jokes.setup: Girl reading Pride and Prejudice
jokes.punchline: Abed's smooth Don Draper impression with a sin/smoking reference
jokes.explanation: Good Abed impression moment. The wordplay on sins/pride/prejudice is clever and fits Don Draper's style.

jokes.text: Then you picked the wrong outfit. Didn't you?
jokes.setup: Girl saying they can't smoke inside
jokes.punchline: Abed's Don Draper response implying her outfit invited rule-breaking
jokes.explanation: Solid Don Draper impression. The logic is appropriately sleazy and confident in that Mad Men way.

jokes.text: Don draper from mad men. What'd you think? Weird. Awesome. Put your tongue in her ear.
jokes.setup: Abed explaining his character choice
jokes.punchline: The group's mixed reactions, Pierce's inappropriate suggestion
jokes.explanation: Decent reaction beat showing different character perspectives. Pierce's line is predictably inappropriate.

jokes.text: Don't be him. He cheats on his wife. Be somebody nice, like mike brady. He always had that housekeeper throwing herself at him, And he never made a move on her.
jokes.setup: Annie rejecting Don Draper as a role model
jokes.punchline: Her innocent interpretation of Mike Brady resisting Alice's advances
jokes.explanation: Good Annie moment showing her idealistic interpretation of TV shows. The Alice/Mike dynamic reading is funny.

jokes.text: You should be like jo from facts of life. But, you know, the dude version. I knew it.
jokes.setup: Britta suggesting a character for Abed to emulate
jokes.punchline: Jeff's knowing reaction to Britta's choice
jokes.explanation: Jeff recognizing something about Britta in her character choice. Implies understanding of her personality.

jokes.text: You should be like calvin. His best friend was a tiger. He always went on dope adventures. If anything stood in his way, he just peed on it. Calvin coolidge?
jokes.setup: Troy describing Calvin's attributes
jokes.punchline: Jeff thinking he means Calvin Coolidge instead of Calvin from Calvin & Hobbes
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection. Jeff's assumption about historical figures vs. Troy's comic strip reference creates perfect confusion.

jokes.text: 10% dick van dyke, 20% sam malone, 40% zach braff from scrubs, And 30% hilary swank in boys don't cry.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he did his Jeff impression
jokes.punchline: The precise percentage breakdown including an unexpected gender-bending reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Abed moment - his analytical breakdown of performance is both funny and insightful. The Hilary Swank reference is inspired.

jokes.text: Because I don't look cool in shorts! Oh! 'cause I'm not cool in shorts! that was good. Just like him. That was really good.
jokes.setup: Jeff getting worked up about the shorts issue
jokes.punchline: His emotional outburst followed by the group's appreciation of his authenticity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeff's vanity breaking through his composure. The group's reaction adds to it.

jokes.text: I don't want to spoil the ending, but, uh... Pride ends up with prejudice. that's funny. I know. Stick with me, and you'll get a laugh or two. That's the abed guarantee.
jokes.setup: Abed making a Pride and Prejudice joke
jokes.punchline: 'That's the abed guarantee' - breaking character to brand himself
jokes.explanation: Good Abed moment - the Jeff impression works but he can't help but add his own meta commentary. Very characteristic.

jokes.text: Oh, my god. It's a white abed. I-I-I--it's like abed, but employable.
jokes.setup: Seeing Jenny's boyfriend who looks like Abed
jokes.punchline: Troy's casual racism about employment prospects
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective observation about racial dynamics in dating and employment. Troy's casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Jeff winger, I've been informed by coach bogner that you left your panties in his pool class. So better pick those up.
jokes.setup: Dean making announcements
jokes.punchline: Publicly humiliates Jeff by calling his concern about shorts 'panties'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the pool storyline. Dean's passive-aggressive public humiliation fits his character.

jokes.text: When is he gonna stop doing that? Do you know that he called me a hipster! Hipster! I mean, do hipsters walk around... Wearing $300 jeans from Italy?
jokes.setup: Jeff complaining about being called a hipster
jokes.punchline: His defense inadvertently proving he's exactly what he's denying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff moment - his vanity and lack of self-awareness on full display. The expensive jeans detail proves the point.

jokes.text: Well, I hate to say that I told you so. So I'll shout it through cupped hands. I told you so!
jokes.setup: Jeff claiming he doesn't want to say 'I told you so'
jokes.punchline: Immediately doing exactly that with theatrical emphasis
jokes.explanation: Great Jeff moment - the fake reluctance followed by theatrical gloating is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Those are the big three. That's what they call them.
jokes.setup: Annie mentioning love, romance, and vaginas
jokes.punchline: Pierce's matter-of-fact confirmation that these are 'the big three'
jokes.explanation: Decent Pierce moment - his casual authority on inappropriate topics is in character but not exceptional.

jokes.text: So does that make abed 'brown joey'? If you want to get racist about it.
jokes.setup: Learning about White Abed whose name is Joey
jokes.punchline: Abed's casual acknowledgment of the racial dynamics
jokes.explanation: Good Abed moment - his matter-of-fact acknowledgment of racial labeling without being offended about it.

jokes.text: Or they trick me into buying them ice cream... And then shove me into a clothes dryer.
jokes.setup: Abed describing how people usually treat him
jokes.punchline: The specific and oddly vivid example of past bullying
jokes.explanation: Great specific detail that feels both sad and funny. The ice cream trick adds an extra layer of cruelty.

jokes.text: The truth is, lots of girls like me, Because, let's face it, I'm pretty adorable. And my aloofness unconsciously reminds them... Of their fathers, so...
jokes.setup: Abed revealing the truth about his dating life
jokes.punchline: His casual psychoanalysis of why he attracts women (daddy issues)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed - clinical self-analysis with uncomfortable psychological insight. The daddy issues observation is both funny and dark.

jokes.text: Britta, I got sel f-esteem falling out of my butt.
jokes.setup: Britta worried about damaging Abed's self-esteem
jokes.punchline: Abed's colorful assurance that his self-esteem is abundant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed line - the crude metaphor delivered deadpan shows his confidence and unique way of expressing himself.

jokes.text: Because when you really know who you are... And what you like about yourself, Changing for other people isn't such a big deal. Abed... You're a god.
jokes.setup: Abed's wise insight about self-knowledge and change
jokes.punchline: Jeff's awed reaction to Abed's wisdom
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Abed's depth and Jeff's ability to recognize wisdom. Builds their relationship.

jokes.text: Is that code for going number one... Or number two?
jokes.setup: Jeff saying he has a man to beat in pool while wearing shorts
jokes.punchline: Troy interpreting it as bathroom terminology
jokes.explanation: Weak Troy moment - the bathroom humor is too obvious and doesn't add much to the scene.

jokes.text: I don't care how I say the word 'baggel.' 'baggel.' She said 'baggel.' Would you like another one? 'baggel.'
jokes.setup: Britta claiming she doesn't care about pronunciation
jokes.punchline: Immediately saying 'baggel' multiple times, proving she still doesn't get it
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Britta remains completely oblivious. Her defiance while making the same mistake is perfect.

jokes.text: Now, do you want to talk about clothes like a girl? Or use tapered sticks to hit balls... Around a cushioned table... Like a man?
jokes.setup: Coach challenging Jeff to focus on pool
jokes.punchline: His overwrought gendered description of pool while obsessing over clothing
jokes.explanation: Decent Coach moment showing his hypocrisy about gender stereotypes while being obsessed with appearance himself.

jokes.text: Stop projecting your obsession with clothes onto me... As an excuse for when I beat you in a game... Of real pool!
jokes.setup: The pool game intensifying
jokes.punchline: Jeff's psychological analysis while stripping down to prove his point
jokes.explanation: Good escalation moment. Jeff's psychological insight while doing exactly what he's accused of is nicely ironic.

jokes.text: Stop projecting your obsession with clothes onto me... As an excuse for when I beat you in a game... Of real pool!
jokes.setup: Coach continuing to focus on appearance
jokes.punchline: Jeff turning the clothing obsession back on the coach
jokes.explanation: Good reversal showing Jeff's growth. The 'projecting' language shows his psychology knowledge.

jokes.text: Three to one against winger. Who's on?
jokes.setup: Jeff and coach's escalating conflict
jokes.punchline: Leonard treating it like a sports bet
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Leonard's gambling problem while showing community college atmosphere.

jokes.text: But years from now, when the story's retold, All they're gonna remember... Is one of us loved clothes. And the other loved... The game.
jokes.setup: The climactic moment of their pool battle
jokes.punchline: Coach's overwrought speech about legacy while both are nearly naked
jokes.explanation: Good climactic moment. Coach's dramatic speech is appropriately overwrought for the absurd situation they've created.

jokes.text: I choose shorts. I choose shorts! Shorts! you son of a bitch. You magnificent son of a bitch!
jokes.setup: Jeff winning and choosing to wear shorts
jokes.punchline: Coach's emotional breakdown and grudging respect
jokes.explanation: Good resolution to the pool conflict. Coach's emotional swing from defeat to admiration works well.

jokes.text: Man...Why couldn't I be brown joey?
jokes.setup: Watching Abed successfully talk to a girl
jokes.punchline: Troy's envious reference to the earlier racial labeling joke
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that shows Troy's envy of Abed's success. The racial dynamics add complexity to their friendship.
